title: Chip shop curry sauce
subtitle: The mild, sweet golden sauce poured over chips in British chip shops
excerpt: Chip shop curry sauce is not the same as Indian curry sauce. It is mild, slightly sweet, and golden, thickened with onion and flour, and designed to pour over a portion of chips. It takes 15 minutes to make and tastes exactly right.
sourceNotes: British chip shop tradition from the 1970s. Curry sauce became a chip shop staple across England, particularly in the North, as a mild, accessible alternative to gravy. The sauce is distinctly British and differs substantially from Indian curry sauces in flavour and method.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze in portions for up to 3 months. Reheat gently, adding a splash of water if the sauce has thickened.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make up to 3 days ahead and keep chilled. Reheat gently before serving.

content.text: The secret of chip shop curry sauce is that it is not a complex sauce. It is built on softened onion, mild curry powder, a little flour to thicken, and vegetable stock. The apple adds the characteristic slight sweetness. It should be quite thin: thick enough to coat the chips but loose enough to pour. Blending is optional but gives a smoother result.
Melt the butter in a medium saucepan over a medium heat. Add the onion and cook for 8 minutes until soft and lightly golden. Add the garlic, curry powder, and turmeric and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the flour and cook for 1 minute. Add the stock gradually, stirring, then add the grated apple. Simmer for 8 to 10 minutes until the sauce has thickened slightly and the apple has broken down.
Blend with a stick blender for a smooth sauce, or leave it as it is for a more textured version. Season with salt. Pour generously over chips to serve.

items.cause: Too much flour or reduced too long.
items.fix: Add a splash more stock and stir. Chip shop curry sauce should pour easily, not sit in a mound.

items.symptom: The sauce tastes too sharp or spicy
items.cause: Curry powder was too hot, or too much was used.
items.fix: Use a mild curry powder labelled specifically as mild. Add a small extra pinch of sugar or a bit more grated apple to balance.
The golden colour comes from turmeric. Add another quarter teaspoon if the sauce looks pale.

content.text: Chip shop curry sauce became popular in the 1970s, especially in the North of England, the Midlands, and Scotland. It is not an Indian curry sauce adapted for chips. It is a British invention, built on mild curry powder that had been in British kitchens since the Victorian era. It now sits on every chip shop menu, served alongside gravy as the two great options for topping a portion of chips.
